problem_statement: Small-scale artisan goat cheese producers in Argentine Patagonia struggle with inventory management and regulatory traceability, relying on manual paper records or generic spreadsheets that are error-prone and time-consuming, while existing software solutions are designed for larger dairy operations and are too expensive or complex for family-scale producers.
target_user: Small-scale artisan goat cheese producers in Argentine Patagonia (85% family-type operations, largely informal, with minimal technical infrastructure)
evidence_summary: 6 unique complaint threads found across cheese forums, dairy industry publications, and Argentine agricultural media. Pattern: small artisan producers face record-keeping pain (manual processes, errors, regulatory gaps) but complaint volume is low — the niche is too small to generate significant online discussion. 85% of Patagonian caprine producers are informal family operations with only 4% of caprine stock formally declared via electronic transit documents. Existing paid solutions (6+) target medium-to-large dairy operations, not micro-artisanal producers.
